docs.title: ACXWA-CD
docs.text: Chassis: ACXWA-CD Brand/Manufacturer: ACXWA Release: 2024, June Wheelbase: 90–118 mm Track width: 69–77 mm (up to 84 mm with long tie-rod)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
At first, the ACXWA introduced itself as a front end conversion for Wltoys K9 in 2023.

Then during 2024, the ACXWA-CD chassis was developed and released. At first, the community was a bit suspicious because of the price, but then it was well adored as a good value for money kit.

docs.title: Technical Specifications
docs.text:
• Motor position: High / High-Rear
  • Servo type: Micro servo
  • Steering: Direct drive
  • Chassis material: Nylon, stainless steel and carbon fiber
  • Suspension: Double wishbone
  • Diff / Gearbox: Solid axle / Belt driven

docs.title: TG Super FIRE
docs.text: Chassis: TG Super FIRE Brand/Manufacturer: TG Release: 2023, February Wheelbase: 94–108mm (Up to 122mm with OP) Track width: 72–85mm (Up to 90mm with OP)

docs.title: Technical Specifications
docs.text:
• Motor position: Mid / High / Rear
  • Chassis material: Aluminum, stainless steel and carbon fiber
  • Suspension: Double wishbone / Full metal shock absorbers
  • Diff / Gearbox: Solid axle / 3 Metal gears gearbox

docs.title: How RWD came to small scale drifting
2014 Early experiments – Afada Rioo
docs.text:
Imagine searching by keyword RWD at all mini rc groups by year until there's no results, and the earliest i found it mentioned was 2014. But imagine that at the group Mini R/C Drift Hobby Indonesia, everything according to RWD was either made or posted by Afada Rioo. He was a pioneer at the time! Going trough the posts, i felt the anticipation of each next post of Afada Rioo by the community. He challenged himself and accepted his own challenge! Not by any means of today's standard, but still...he made it, and not only by making his project work, but more importantly, by inspiring everyone else. I recall going through his posts, it seemed like he was explaining rocket science. It was different than it's now. He couldn't just buy precise nano electronics, because there weren't any. Just look at this.

The following is created later i think, but seriously....what is this?

He had to learn so much in all aspects and do many experiments, change the way some electronics work and he did it! Today we can say that he's the grandfather of small scale rwd drifting, and it's really related in interesting way.

docs.title: 2016 Legowo Setiawan and the first K989 conversion
docs.text:
Legowo Setiawan was into 1/10 rc drift and he studied the constructions and how stuff worked there. In 2015 he decided to to use what he's learnd and some of Afada Rioo's discoveries and started his work on rwd project. During 2016, he managed to convert Wltoys K989 to RWD drift car. Inspired to go even further, In 2017 he successfully converted Atomic AMR to RWD drifter and that didn't go unnoticed.

I told you Afada Rioo's work was important. Now look at this two cars together.

Legowo received an endorsement from Atomic. The manager, mr.Kim, started developing a kit based on his build. Legowo made two prototype kits: one of them, he sent to Atomic, and the other, he continued developing himself. According to mr.Setiawan, DRZ1 and DRZ2 are result from his research with Atomic, but at the end of 2018, Atomic unilaterally ended the contract with him and still haven't responded to his last message. Hearing both sides would be valuable for historical accuracy, but mr.Kim haven't replyed to me either. Yet one thing is certain. It's mr.Legowo Setiawan and mr.Kim that are responsible for the existence of the first dedicated small scale, rear wheel drive, rc drift chassis.

docs.title: 2018 Birth of the Atomic DRZ
docs.text:
-On 06 January 2018, Atomic announced the DRZ on their facebook page.

-Sales started during march.

Some people found out about it from Atomic, RcMart, DriftaHolics RC, Mini-Z groups, but still small scale wasn't that popular.

docs.title: 2018 Some hobbyists started to develop their own diy rwd's and designs.
docs.text:
13 January 2018 Andi Capitt posted MINI-Z RWD kit:

26 July 2018 Pebysetyana has posted this:

Later in October 2018 he introduced BRP.

Then BRPV2 December 2018

More and more Wltoys K9 RWD conversions started to appear.

docs.title: May 2019 XRX Techstorm DPA
docs.text: XRX came to the game firing branded electronic components and OP parts made of 7075 T6 anodized aluminum like a machine gun.

docs.title: June 2019 Super Skeeter conversion by BMHobby (BM Racing)
docs.text:
• At 12 June, the Super Skeeter conversion by BM racing was released, offering:
  • steering crank direct drive with servo.
  • easily wheel base adjustment.
  • caster quick adjustment.
  • Ackermann angle adjustment.

It was shiny, it was good, everyone involved wanted one back then. How not to want one? Look at it!

docs.title: COVID-19 and the boom of home drifting
docs.text:
When the COVID-19 pandemic hit in early 2020, almost every RC drift track in the world closed overnight. For the 1/10 scale scene, this was a major setback — but for small scale drifting, it unexpectedly became a catalyst.

People were stuck at home with limited space, limited budget, and a lot of time. Many hobbyists who previously drove only 1/10 cars suddenly looked for something they could use indoors, on small layouts, even on their living room floor.

Small scale RWD drift fit perfectly into that gap.

Social media groups started filling with homemade tracks made from: - EVA foam mats - cardboard barriers - wooden tiles - even whiteboards or polished tables

The creativity exploded.

Online communities grew rapidly. Build logs, setup experiments, DIY chassis mods and custom electronics were being shared at a pace never seen before. For the first time, drifting went from being “track-based” to being a “home lab” hobby.

And because small scale cars consume very little space and parts are more affordable, newcomers felt no barrier to entry. People who never considered RC drifting before suddenly got into it — and stayed.

COVID-19 didn’t just push the hobby forward. It worldwide accelerated small scale RWD drifting into a full ecosystem of: - independent designers - home builders - tuning experiments - 3D printed chassis - specialized electronics - small local communities

It was the moment the niche became a movement.

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
Small scale RWD drifting did not appear out of nowhere. It was shaped by a handful of passionate individuals experimenting in their homes, sharing their discoveries online, and pushing the limits of what was possible in miniature scale.

From Afada Rioo’s pioneering attempts in 2014, to Legowo Setiawan’s creative conversions, to Atomic’s DRZ bringing a dedicated RWD chassis to the world — every step built on the previous one. Independent designers, new conversion kits, and later XRX and BMHobby’s contributions expanded the scene even further.

By the time the pandemic hit, the foundation was already there — and the global shift to indoor hobbies finally allowed small scale RWD drifting to bloom.

This history is not just about chassis and electronics. It’s about creativity, curiosity, and a community that grew from a few experiments into a worldwide movement.
